topic.what: DL-based explainable dynamic analysis framework for detecting malicious PyPI packages using eBPF-based behavioral monitoring
topic.how: Captures install-time and post-installation behavioral traces via eBPF, applies FLAML-based feature selection and DL models with stability and explainability analysis
topic.why_matters: Addresses next-gen software supply chain attacks targeting PyPI with multiphase malware execution, remote access activation, and dynamic payload generation that evade static and metadata-based detectors

characteristics.what_it_measures: Classification accuracy for benign vs malicious packages
characteristics.context: MLP model on QUT-DV25 dataset with 17 selected features

characteristics.what_it_measures: Benign packages incorrectly classified as malicious
characteristics.context: Relative reduction compared to prior state-of-the-art

characteristics.what_it_measures: Malicious packages incorrectly classified as benign
characteristics.context: Relative reduction compared to prior state-of-the-art

characteristics.what_it_measures: Number of features required for detection

characteristics.context: Per-package inference time on test set

characteristics.vs_baseline: vs prior studies with qualitative stability
characteristics.context: Standard deviation, confidence intervals, and rank-based comparison

novelty: First DL-based explainable framework for PyPI dynamic analysis
Integration of stability analysis with quantitative metrics (std, rank, CI)
FLAML-based feature selection achieving 52.78% dimensionality reduction
SHAP and LIME for per-package model interpretation
Multi-phase model comparison (classical, pre-attention, attention-based DL)
open_problems: Handling sophisticated obfuscation and evasion techniques
Real-time continuous monitoring at package registry scale
Adaptation to emerging AI-generated malicious packages
Long-term model maintenance as attack patterns evolve
